title: School Admin — Announcements & Calendar
content:
Overview


The Announcements and Calendar pages are the school's communication tools. Announcements allow admins to broadcast messages to specific audiences. The Calendar tracks school-wide events, exams, holidays, and breaks.



Announcements — Creating an Announcement


  1. Click New Announcement at the top of the Announcements page
  2. Enter a Title (max 100 characters)
  3. Enter the Message (max 1000 characters) — a character counter shows remaining space
  4. Select the target audience from the options below
  5. Click Post Announcement

Targeting Options:


  • Everyone: All users in the school — students, teachers, parents, and admins
  • All Students: Only students enrolled at the school
  • All Teachers: Only teachers registered at the school
  • By Grade: Select one or more specific grade levels — only students in those grades and their linked parents will see the announcement
  • By Subject: Select one or more subjects — only students enrolled in those subjects and their teachers will see it
  • Specific Person: Search for and select an individual student, teacher, or parent

When a targeting option is selected, additional dropdowns or search fields appear to refine the audience. The form dynamically updates based on the chosen target type.



Announcements — Managing Existing Announcements


All active announcements are listed in a table below the creation form. Key features:


  • Search: Filter announcements by title or content
  • View: See the title, target audience, date posted, and a preview of the message
  • Edit: Click the edit icon to modify the title or message of an existing announcement
  • Delete: Remove an announcement that is no longer relevant

Edited announcements retain their original posting date. Deleted announcements are permanently removed and cannot be recovered.



Calendar — Adding an Event


  1. Click Add Event at the top of the Calendar page
  2. Enter the Title (max 100 characters) — required
  3. Select the Event Type from the dropdown:
    • Exam / Test: For midterms, finals, standardized tests
    • Holiday: School closures for public holidays
    • Break: Spring break, winter break, summer vacation
    • School Event: Assemblies, parent-teacher conferences, sports days
    • Other: Any other notable date
  4. Set the Start Date and End Date — required. For single-day events, start and end dates can be the same
  5. Add optional Notes (max 300 characters) for additional details
  6. Click Save Event


Calendar — Viewing Events


The calendar is divided into two sections:


  • Upcoming & Current: All events that are happening today or in the future, sorted by date. Shows the event title, type (with color-coded icon), date range, and notes. Events can be edited or deleted from this section.
  • Past Events: Collapsed by default — click the header to expand. Shows all events that have already ended. A Clear All Past button allows bulk deletion of past events.

Each event card displays an icon based on its type: exam, holiday, break, event, or other — making the calendar visually scannable.



Where Announcements and Calendar Appear


  • Announcements: Visible to students on their Announcements page (read-only), to teachers on their Announcements page (read-only), and to parents on their Announcements page (read-only). Only admins can create or edit.
  • Calendar: Visible to students, teachers, and parents on their respective Calendar pages (read-only). Only admins can create, edit, or delete events.


Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "You can target announcements to specific grades, subjects, or even individual people — use the radio buttons to choose."
  • "Announcements are read-only for teachers, students, and parents. Only admins can create or edit them."
  • "Calendar events can span multiple days — set the start and end dates for things like exam week or spring break."
  • "Past events are hidden by default but you can expand them to review or clear them."
  • "If a teacher says they can't post an announcement, that's correct — only school admins have that permission."
